testcase.name: test_project_home_GET

failure.message: ValueError: Cannot assign "'1'": "Post.author" must be a "User" instance.

testcase.failure:
self = <app.tests.test_views.TestView testMethod=test_project_home_GET>
def setUp(self): self.client = Client() self.home_url = reverse('website-home') self.detail_url = reverse('post-detail', args='1') user = User.objects.create( username='123', email='pastas@mail.com', password='Testas12345.')
> Post.objects.create( title='post1', content='random post', author = '1')
app\tests\test_views.py:20: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ env1\lib\site-packages\django\db\models\manager.py:85: in manager_method return getattr(self.get_queryset(), name)(*args, **kwargs) env1\lib\site-packages\django\db\models\query.py:669: in create obj = self.model(**kwargs) env1\lib\site-packages\django\db\models\base.py:541: in __init__ _setattr(self, field.name, rel_obj) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <django.db.models.fields.related_descriptors.ForwardManyToOneDescriptor object at 0x000001FFA4421E50> instance = <Post: post1>, value = '1'
def __set__(self, instance, value): """ Set the related instance through the forward relation.
With the example above, when setting ``child.parent = parent``:
- ``self`` is the descriptor managing the ``parent`` attribute - ``instance`` is the ``child`` instance - ``value`` is the ``parent`` instance on the right of the equal sign """ # An object must be an instance of the related class. if value is not None and not isinstance( value, self.field.remote_field.model._meta.concrete_model ): > raise ValueError( 'Cannot assign "%r": "%s.%s" must be a "%s" instance.' % ( value, instance._meta.object_name, self.field.name, self.field.remote_field.model._meta.object_name, ) E ValueError: Cannot assign "'1'": "Post.author" must be a "User" instance.
env1\lib\site-packages\django\db\models\fields\related_descriptors.py:235: ValueError

self = <django.db.models.fields.related_descriptors.ForwardManyToOneDescriptor object at 0x000001FFA4421E50> instance = <Post: post1>, value = '1'
def __set__(self, instance, value): """ Set the related instance through the forward relation.
With the example above, when setting ``child.parent = parent``:
- ``self`` is the descriptor managing the ``parent`` attribute - ``instance`` is the ``child`` instance - ``value`` is the ``parent`` instance on the right of the equal sign """ # An object must be an instance of the related class. if value is not None and not isinstance( value, self.field.remote_field.model._meta.concrete_model ): > raise ValueError( 'Cannot assign "%r": "%s.%s" must be a "%s" instance.' % ( value, instance._meta.object_name, self.field.name, self.field.remote_field.model._meta.object_name, ) E ValueError: Cannot assign "'1'": "Post.author" must be a "User" instance.
